paper_title: Soil Properties and DOM Characteristics in Alaskan Permafrost Soils (Inferred)

methods.description: Collection of soil cores from three distinct sites in interior Alaska representing different permafrost zones and soil types.

steps.description: Soils were collected from three sites in interior Alaska (table 1, figure 1). The most northern site, Coldfoot (67.20 • N, 150.27 • W), is an upland forested site in the continuous permafrost zone. Soils are organic and mineral in the active layer (organic layer thickness = ∼30-35 cm), transitioning to permafrost mineral soils. The Hess Creek site (65.67 • N, 149.08 • W) is located just south of the Yukon River in discontinuous permafrost and is a deep peatland (>3 m peat) having organic active-layer and permafrost soils. The Smith Lake site (64.87 • N, 147.86 • W) is a forested area in Fairbanks, AK, and contains organic and mineral soils in active-layer and permafrost horizons (organic layer thickness = ∼34-45 cm, with spatially variable deeper organic layers). Following USDA Soil Taxonomy (Soil Survey Staff 1999), all soils are Gelisols (permafrost within 1 m of the surface); soils from Coldfoot are Orthels (non-cryoturbated mineral soil suborder), soils from Hess Creek are Histels (organic soils suborder), and soils from Smith Lake are Turbels (cryoturbated soils suborder). We refer to the sites as CF (Coldfoot), HC (Hess Creek), and SL (Smith Lake).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and document soil collection sites and characteristics
outputs: site descriptions (permafrost zone, vegetation, soil type)

steps.description: Soil cores were collected in March when activelayer soils were still frozen (maximum active-layer thicknesses at each sampling site were measured the previous September; Waldrop et al 2010). Three cores were collected from 0-1 m depth at each site within a 100 m 2 area using a SIPRE (Snow, Ice, and Permafrost Research Establishment) corer, and were kept frozen during transport to a −10 • C cold room at Fort Wainwright Army Base in Fairbanks, AK for processing.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect soil cores using a SIPRE corer

methods.description: Processing of collected soil cores, including segmentation and preparation of subsamples for analysis.

steps.description: Soil cores were cut into segments using a band saw, and two 15 cm-thick segments were extracted from each core: a deep active-layer horizon (∼15-30 cm above the permafrost boundary) and a shallow permafrost horizon (∼15-30 cm below the permafrost boundary).
steps.searchable_summary: Segment soil cores and extract specific horizons
outputs: metadata (core ID, segment depth, horizon type)

steps.description: Samples were cut lengthwise and splits were shipped on dry ice to USGS labs in Boulder, CO and Menlo Park, CA and kept frozen until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Split soil segments and ship frozen subsamples

methods.description: Analysis of soil sample splits for basic physical and chemical properties: moisture content, bulk density, C and N content, and radiocarbon content.

steps.description: Moisture content and bulk density were determined by drying a known volume of soil at 65 • C (organic) or 105 • C (mineral) for 48 hours and measuring the weight before and after drying.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate moisture content and bulk density by drying

steps.description: C and N content were measured on a C/N analyzer (CE Elantech, Lakewood, NJ, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure total carbon and nitrogen content

steps.description: Subsamples were sent to the W.M. Keck Accelerator Mass Spectrometry Laboratory at University of California-Irvine for oxidation to CO 2 and CO 2 purification, graphitization, and radiocarbon measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure radiocarbon content using AMS

steps.description: Radiocarbon Δ 14 C (‰), and radiocarbon age as years before present (ybp) were reported.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report radiocarbon delta and age

methods.description: Extraction of dissolved organic matter (DOM) from soil samples by leaching with deionized water.

steps.description: A second set of soil sample splits were leached by placing pre-weighed frozen soils in glass beakers with 500 ml of deionized water and allowing them to thaw at 5 • C in the dark for 24 hours on shaker tables.
steps.searchable_summary: Leach frozen soil samples with deionized water

steps.description: The mass ratio of field-moist soil to water (g g −1 ) ranged from 0.22-0.56 and averaged 0.32 (n = 18), similar to the suggested mass ratio for soil: extractant of ≥0.25 for directly comparable DOC and DON recovery from soils (Jones and Willett 2006, table S1 available at stacks.iop.org/ERL/13/065011/mmedia).
steps.searchable_summary: Document soil to water mass ratio

steps.description: Soil-water slurries were decanted and filtered using pre-combusted GF/F filters.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter soil-water slurries

steps.description: DOC, TDN, DIN (nitrate plus ammonium), and DON (TDN minus DIN) yields normalized to total dry soil weight were calculated using solute concentrations (analytical methods described below) and accounting for soil water volume and added leachate water volume (table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate normalized yields of DOC, TDN, DIN, and DON

steps.description: A small volume of unfiltered water was saved as inoculum for biodegradable DOC (BDOC) incubations, described below.
steps.searchable_summary: Save unfiltered leachate as inoculum

methods.description: Analysis of filtered leachate samples for concentrations of dissolved organic and inorganic nitrogen species and dissolved organic matter (DOM) optical properties.

steps.description: Leachate samples were analyzed within 48 h for concentrations of DOC (OI700 analyzer, O.I. Analytical), TDN (Shimadzu Total Nitrogen analyzer), and nitrate (NO 3 − ) and ammonium (NH 4 + ) (Dionex Model DX-120 Ion Chromatograph), and for DOM optical properties.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure concentrations of DOC, TDN, nitrate, and ammonium in leachate

steps.description: UV-visible absorbance was measured using a Hewlett-Packard Model 8453 photo-diode array spectrophotometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure UV-Vis absorbance spectrum of leachate

steps.description: Specific UV absorbance (SUVA 254 ), an index of DOC aromatic-ity (Weishaar et al 2003), was determined by dividing absorbance at = 254 nm (UV 254 ) by DOC concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate SUVA254 from UV absorbance and DOC concentration

steps.description: Spectral slope ratio (S R ), an indicator of DOM molecular weight, was calculated as the ratio of the slopes of adsorption between = 275-295 nm (S 275−295 ) and = 350-400 nm (S 350−400 ) (Helms et al 2008).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate spectral slope ratio (SR) from UV absorbance spectrum

steps.description: Fluorescence excitation-emission matrices (EEMs) were measured using a Horiba-JY Fluoromax-3 spectrofluorometer as described in Wickland et al (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure fluorescence excitation-emission matrices (EEMs)

steps.description: Fluorescence Index (FI;McKnight et al 2001), which is used to distinguish between microbially-derived (FI = 1.7-2.0) and higher plant-derived (FI < 1.4) DOM fulvic acids, was calculated as the ratio of the intensities at excitation (ex) and emission (em) wavelengths ex370/em470 and ex370/em520 (Cory et al 2010).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Fluorescence Index (FI) from EEMs

steps.description: Humification Index (HIX; Ohno 2002) was calculated as the ratio of the sum of the intensities at ex255/em434-480 divided by the sum of intensities at ex255/em300-344 and ex255/em434-480. HIX values can range from 0-1, with higher values indicating an increasing degree of humification of DOM (Ohno 2002).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Humification Index (HIX) from EEMs

steps.description: DOC-normalized fluorescence intensities of six fluorescence ex/em peaks associated with various DOM pools (Kraus et al 2008) were determined. These peaks include B (ex270/em310; tyrosine-like, proteinlike fluorophore), T (ex260/em340; tryptophan-like, protein-like fluorophore), A (ex260/em450; humiclike), C (ex330/em446; humic-like), D (ex370/em500; soil fulvic acid), and E (ex450/em520; soil fulvic acid) peaks.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine DOC-normalized intensities of specific fluorescence peaks from EEMs
outputs: DOC-normalized peak intensities (B, T, A, C, D, E)

steps.description: We grouped peaks into 'protein-like' (B, T) and 'humic-like + fulvic-like' (A, C, D, E) and summed their intensities.
steps.searchable_summary: Group and sum specific fluorescence peak intensities
inputs: DOC-normalized peak intensities (B, T, A, C, D, E)

methods.description: Determination of the biodegradable fraction of dissolved organic carbon (BDOC) through microbial incubation and measurement of dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) production.

steps.description: Biodegradable DOC (BDOC) content was determined using methods described in Wickland et al (2007Wickland et al ( , 2012, where inoculated samples (1% 1.6 mfiltered inoculant derived from the soil leachates) were incubated in serum bottles with 50% headspace by volume and dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) production was measured over time.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate leachate samples with inoculum and measure DIC production over time
inputs: diluted leachate samples (<10 mg DOC L-1)

steps.description: The difference between initial and final total DIC concentrations was assumed to result entirely from DOC mineralization (McDowell et al 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate BDOC as the difference between initial and final DIC concentrations

methods.description: Statistical procedures applied to analyze differences in soil properties and leachate yields, and relationships between variables.

steps.description: Analysis of variance (General Linear Model Nested Design ANOVA) was used to determine significant differences in soil properties and leachate yields among sites and thermal horizon (active layer vs. permafrost), followed by Tukey's HSD post hoc test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform nested ANOVA and Tukey's HSD post hoc test

steps.description: Regression analysis and General Linear Model (GLM) Separate Slopes Model were used to test for significant differences in the relationships between DOC, TDN, DON or DIN yields and soil properties (%C, %N, Δ 14 C) for active-layer and permafrost soils.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform regression analysis and GLM Separate Slopes Model

steps.description: Regression analysis was used to test for significance between % BDOC and various parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform regression analysis between % BDOC and other parameters
inputs: various parameters (e.g., soil properties, leachate characteristics)
